close_reason: recompute bare-module head-union hashes in compute_tier_hashes(:api_boundary, ...) to prevent bare module entries from dropping on clean runs
description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

The silent-seed pass writes bare-module api_boundary entries (Canonical.hash_module_head_union), but compute_tier_hashes(:api_boundary,...) in refresh_and_commit_hashes skips bare modules ({:ok, {:module, _}} -> acc) and HashStore.write has replacement semantics. So a clean (no-drift) run drops all bare-module api_boundary entries from realization_hashes.json, and the next run silently re-seeds them from the current tree. Two consequences: (1) the committed baseline has two different fixed points depending on whether the last committing run had drift — observed on main where a4bfd3b (committed under drift) carried 15 bare-module entries that the first clean run dropped; (2) cross-commit drift detection for bare modules is effectively dead whenever the committed baseline came from a clean run, because a checkout with a drifted bare module just re-seeds it at the drifted value. refresh should recompute bare-module head-union hashes instead of skipping them (or seed should not write them under api_boundary).

comments.text: PR opened: https://github.com/iautom8things/specled_ex/pull/7 (release 0.3.1). Ticket was already closed; PR carries the fix + review remediations. Babysitting CI.

Squash-merged to main (b6c8015) as release 0.3.1. Post-merge Specs run green (29999265337). Worktree + branches cleaned. Follow-ups: specled_-oyg (attestation coverage), new ticket for worktree-cleanup force path.

title: bare-module api_boundary entries oscillate between seed and refresh passes
